topic: A college student discovers their roommate has been reading their journal
raw_output:
[story 1]

The silence in the room had become a thick, suffocating blanket. I was halfway through detailing my anxieties about my upcoming presentation, the ink still fresh on the page, when I glanced up. Liam was sitting on his bed, ostensibly studying, but his gaze wasn't on his textbook. It was fixed on my desk. My heart gave a sudden, unpleasant lurch. I slowly lowered my pen, my fingers stiffening around the paper. He didn't look away immediately. A slow, almost imperceptible smile touched the corner of his mouth. When he finally shifted his attention back to his notes, his eyes held a strange, knowing glint that made the air feel thin. I cleared my throat, a dry, rasping sound. "Everything alright?" I managed, my voice sounding unnaturally high. He just blinked, a deliberate, slow movement, and offered a noncommittal shrug that managed to convey volumes of unspoken understanding. I felt a sudden, intense need to check the lock on my door, though I knew it was unnecessary.

[story 2]

I found the evidence tucked beneath my history notes: a page from my private journal, the handwriting unmistakably mine, annotated with Liam’s neat, precise script. My breath hitched, catching somewhere deep in my chest. I stared at the page, the mundane script suddenly transformed into something charged and secretive. I stood up so abruptly my chair scraped loudly against the floorboards. Liam was across the room, engrossed in a video game, his headphones clamped over his ears. I walked over, my steps hesitant, and stopped just short of his peripheral vision. "What is this?" I asked, my voice barely a whisper, laced with a tremor I couldn't control. He didn't jump. He simply pulled one headphone off, letting it dangle around his neck, and met my eyes. His expression was utterly blank, a smooth, unreadable surface that made my stomach clench with a confusing mixture of alarm and bewildered curiosity.

[story 3]

It was the way the light hit the page that first alerted me. A shadow, a slight darkening of the paper where a finger had rested too long. I crept closer, my bare feet silent on the worn carpet. My hands were clammy as I leaned over the desk, my entire focus narrowed to the illicit text. I saw the marginalia—notes I hadn't even realized I’d written, observations about my dreams, fragments of conversations I’d only shared with myself. When I looked up, Liam was standing by the doorway, leaning against the frame, watching me with an unnerving stillness. He didn't speak, didn't move to hide anything. He just stood there, arms crossed loosely over his chest, and the quiet tension between us stretched taut, vibrating in the small space. I couldn't form a coherent sentence; all I could do was stare, my mouth slightly agape.
